key_insight: Materials Spatial Intelligence (MSI) learns directly from multimodal, high-resolution microstructural data to predict properties while preserving the spatial relationships that reveal governing mechanisms.
engineering_relevance: The framework can help engineers identify which microstructural features control strength–property trade-offs and guide microstructure optimization for structural alloys.
method: Encode multimodal microstructural and deformation observations into shared latent representations.
Preserve spatial organization rather than collapsing data into handcrafted descriptors or pooled features.
Use the learned representation for property prediction, interpretation, and feature-driven optimization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports application to structural alloys, where MSI identifies key governing features and property trade-offs, supporting both prediction and mechanism discovery.
watch_item: The approach depends on high-resolution multimodal spatial data, so performance and interpretability may be limited when data are sparse, noisy, or not well aligned across modalities.
paper_title: Interpretable Material Spatial Intelligence for Discovery of Governing Microstructural Features
